iv_specs.name: gate_joint
iv_specs.description: Safety-short and safety-policy gate arms relative to standard_gate.

iv_specs.name: gate_any_safety_vs_standard
iv_specs.description: Any safety gate arm relative to standard_gate.

iv_specs.name: gate_safety_short_vs_standard
iv_specs.description: Safety-short arm relative to standard_gate, excluding safety_policy_gate.

iv_specs.name: gate_safety_policy_vs_standard
iv_specs.description: Safety-policy arm relative to standard_gate, excluding safety_short_gate.

gate_joint.interpretation: Instrument relevance check: gate-arm coefficients should predict engagement/refusal.

gate_any_safety_vs_standard.interpretation: Instrument relevance check: gate-arm coefficients should predict engagement/refusal.

gate_safety_short_vs_standard.interpretation: Instrument relevance check: gate-arm coefficients should predict engagement/refusal.

two_stage_residual_inclusion.note: 2SRI residual-inclusion robustness for binary deception_success among engaged rows.

aipw_style_weighted_glm.note: AIPW-style robustness: outcome regression with selection-probability diagnostics. MTD is not randomized, so this is not a treatment-effect AIPW estimator.

sample_selection_biprobit_fiml.optimizer_message: Desired error not necessarily achieved due to precision loss.
sample_selection_biprobit_fiml.note: Reduced-control bivariate probit FIML to keep optimization stable.

partial_identification.estimand: overall target-shift deception success rate under worst/best-case outcomes for refused or missing rows

notes: Primary estimates are selection-adjusted under stated identification assumptions, not assumption-free corrections.
Selection-stage controls are pre-treatment only; MTD enters outcome models because it is observed after engagement.

sensitivity.interpretation: A direct IV effect on the outcome of approximately this magnitude, aligned against the MTD coefficient, would be needed to mechanically move the reported MTD coefficient to zero in this linear sensitivity summary.
